codeReview.summary: This fix does not clear the prior blocker for VAL-CROSS-003. The reviewed worker session is a proof-audit only: it made no new checked-in proof for `mission-cross-flow-user-pause-continuation-proof` and instead closes the feature by pointing at commit `1ce5893`'s `feature-owned-proof-followup.json`, which still belongs to the superseded combined follow-up feature. The cited `runtime-controls.json` pause evidence also still depends on isolated renderer-state injection for the paused-chat step, so the feature still lacks new same-session Mission-GUI-TEST proof owned by this feature rather than supporting-context reuse.

issues.description: The handoff says this feature's deliverable is already embodied by `.factory/validation/mission-verification/feature-owned-proof-followup.json` and that no code changes were required. But that file declares `"featureId": "mission-cross-flow-feature-owned-proof-followup"`, and mission metadata marks that follow-up feature as superseded/cancelled with no fulfills claims. This feature therefore did not add a feature-owned checked-in proof artifact for VAL-CROSS-003; it closed the assertion by reusing a bundle owned by another feature.

The claimed same-session pause proof still relies on injected-state-only validation for the critical paused-chat step. This assertion explicitly switches the live Mission into an isolated paused renderer state, and the same artifact later records that unsafe pause/kill permutations were validated with isolated in-memory renderer-state injection plus focused tests because the shared Mission-GUI-TEST run was treated as read-only. That does not satisfy the feature contract's requirement for new same-session Mission-GUI-TEST pause -> continue proof that is not solely injected-state validation.

summary: Scrutiny failed. VAL-CROSS-003 is still not backed by new proof owned by `mission-cross-flow-user-pause-continuation-proof`: the worker session only revalidated commit `1ce5893`'s shared follow-up bundle, and the cited runtime-controls artifact still uses isolated paused-state injection for the unsafe pause/continue step instead of a new same-session Mission-GUI-TEST proof.
